commit.message:
fix: discover shipyard ports when over fleet target

Previously knownShipyardPortIds started empty, so the relocation block
never tagged any ships (gated on knownYards.length > 0) — chicken-and-egg.

Now: when over target, probe up to 20 unique docked-ship port IDs in
parallel each cycle to find shipyards. Discovered ports are added to
knownShipyardPortIds so relocation tagging and direct sells can proceed.
Ports already in the known list are skipped.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: return optimistic fleetTarget in setFleetTarget response

Dashboard sees the new target immediately without waiting for the worker
to pick it up from the command doc.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

debug: add fleet management sell/relocation logging

- [fleetMgmt] logs ships/target/overTarget on every cycle
- [fleetMgmt:sell] logs candidates count, each ship attempted, success/fail with error
- [fleetMgmt:sell] skipped path logs canSell + fleet size gate
- [runCycle:reloc] logs fleet size vs target and known yards count before tagging

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: route fleetTarget through command doc so worker picks it up

Instead of writing fleetTarget to autopilot_states (which the worker
overwrites every cycle), write it to autopilot_commands. The standalone
already polls that collection every 2s and applies changes to its
in-memory state — fleetTarget now follows the same path as enabled/fleetMgmt.

- AutopilotCommand: add fleetTarget?: number | null
- saveAutopilotCommandFleetTarget(): new helper
- autopilotManager.setFleetTarget: writes to command doc only
- checkCommands: reads cmd.fleetTarget, applies to state.fleetMgmt.fleetTarget

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: fleet size target — input, relocation logic, Cog-first priority

Dashboard:
- FleetTargetInput component in the autopilot strip: number input + Set
  button, shows current/target count with ⚠ over-target indicator
- Saves via POST /api/autopilot { fleetTarget } → persisted to MongoDB

API / manager:
- autopilot route accepts fleetTarget in POST body
- autopilotManager.setFleetTarget writes to autopilot state in MongoDB
- useAutopilot exposes setFleetTarget callback

Types:
- FleetMgmtState.fleetTarget?: number
- AutopilotShipState.relocatingToPortId?: string

Autopilot logic:
- COG_TYPE_PATTERN = /cog/i — Cogs sorted first for sell & relocation
- When ships.length > fleetTarget:
    - Sell loop skips the SELL_IDLE_CYCLES requirement (sell any idle ship
      at a known shipyard immediately); Cogs prioritised
    - Relocation tagging runs after getPathsFrom is available (runCycle),
      tags idle non-ferry ships at non-shipyard ports toward the nearest
      known shipyard port; Cogs first
    - Buy loop skipped entirely when over target
- When ships.length <= fleetTarget: stale relocation tags cleared
- Relocation dispatch: Pass 2 checks ss.relocatingToPortId before normal
  routing; dispatches toward the tagged shipyard port and clears on arrival

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: enforce £1.5M treasury floor before cargo buying

- MIN_TREASURY_FLOOR = 1_500_000
- bankingCap = max(MIN_TREASURY_FLOOR, total_upkeep * 2 + 2000)
  so available funds for cargo purchases is always at least £1.5M below
  the current treasury — autopilot stops buying cargo if it would dip
  the treasury under the floor

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

chore: improve project configuration and add CI

- Add description and license to MIT in package.json
- Set private to false
- Add .env* to .gitignore
- Add GitHub Actions CI workflow (pnpm/Vite)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
config: deprioritise ship buying to maximise profit from existing fleet

- BUY_RESERVE_MULTIPLIER: 3x → 20x ship cost (keeps treasury for trading capital)
- BUY_COOLDOWN_MS: 5 min → 1 hour (buys very infrequently)
- Profit gate: 2x → 10x per-cycle upkeep before a buy is even considered

Sell logic unchanged — idle ships are still recycled normally.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: intercept ESM import of node:sqlite in ESM loader to fix CLI subprocess crash on Node.js v20 (#77)

* Initial plan

* fix: intercept ESM import of node:sqlite in ESM loader to fix CLI subprocess crash on Node.js v20

The CJS polyfill (node-sqlite-polyfill.cjs) patched Module._load but ESM
imports bypass Module._load entirely, going through the ESM builtinStrategy
which threw ERR_UNKNOWN_BUILTIN_MODULE for node:sqlite on Node.js v20.

- Add scripts/sqlite-polyfill-esm.mjs: ESM shim exporting DatabaseSync
  backed by better-sqlite3 (API-compatible with node:sqlite)
- Update scripts/esm-ext-loader.mjs: intercept ESM 'node:sqlite' specifier
  in resolve hook on Node.js < v22.5.0 and redirect to the shim file
- Update agentic-demo.yml: document ESM node:sqlite interception in comments
- Update copilot-compat-test.yml: add sqlite-polyfill-esm.mjs to paths
  trigger and add ESM node:sqlite test step

Co-authored-by: rleme <4821673+rleme@users.noreply.github.com>

* style: use .slice(0,2) when parsing Node.js version in ESM loader

Co-authored-by: rleme <4821673+rleme@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: rleme <4821673+rleme@users.noreply.github.com>

Merge open PRs: resolve PR #73 conflict (superseded by #74) (#75)

* Initial plan

commit.message:
fix: deduplicate ledger entries by id on fetch

Upstream ledger endpoint can return the same entry twice, causing React
duplicate key warnings on LedgerRow.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: deduplicate ships by ID after fetchAllPages

Upstream cursor pagination can return the same ship on multiple pages.
Deduplicate in both /api/dashboard/ships and cachedShips() in the worker
so React key collisions and duplicate dispatch attempts don't occur.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: background KPI/ships fetch with skeleton loading + server cache

- New GET /api/dashboard/kpis: reads autopilot state from MongoDB,
  computes cargoInTransitValue / paxInTransit / cargoAtCost / netProfit
  — no upstream API calls, near-instant response

- New GET /api/dashboard/ships?page=N&limit=50: server-side paginated
  ships list with 18s in-memory cache (one cache entry per companyId).
  ~9 upstream paginated calls happen server-to-server once, then all
  browser requests within 18s return cached JSON. Clicking Next/Prev
  pages through the cache without hitting the upstream at all.

- Dashboard initial load now only fetches fast/small data (company,
  economy, ledger, ports, goods, warehouse stocks) — no more blocking
  fetchAllPages for 847 ships on mount

- KPI cards (Cargo, PAX, Net Profit, Total Assets) show animate-pulse
  skeleton until /api/dashboard/kpis resolves in the background

- Ship table shows 8 skeleton rows while /api/dashboard/ships loads,
  then renders real data. Prev/Next buttons disabled while loading.

- SSE ship_docked / ship_transit_started events update shipMeta
  traveling/docked counts in real time

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Reduce logging redundancy

Co-authored-by: Copilot Autofix powered by AI <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add og:image meta tag to bench pages when image is available (#12)

Bench detail pages lacked `og:image` metadata, preventing rich link
previews when sharing bench URLs on social platforms.

## Changes

- **`Layout.astro`**: Added optional `ogImage` prop; renders `<meta
property="og:image">` in `<head>` when set
- **`[siteId].astro`**: Passes `site.benchImageUrl` as `ogImage` to
`<Page>` (which already spreads all props to `Layout`)

```astro
<!-- [siteId].astro -->
<Page title={site.name} ogImage={site.benchImageUrl}>
```

```astro
<!-- Layout.astro -->
{ogImage && <meta property="og:image" content={ogImage} />}
```

commit.message:
config: window 50→20 ships, cycle 20s→10s, ships TTL 25s→8s

- SHIP_WINDOW_SIZE: 50 → 20 (fewer inventory/transit calls per cycle)
- CYCLE_MS: 20s → 10s (faster per-ship throughput across the fleet)
- SELL_IDLE_CYCLES: 9 → 18 (maintains 3-min idle-before-sell threshold)
- SHIPS_TTL_MS: 25s → 8s (still avoids back-to-back re-fetch within a cycle)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: reduce rate-limit pressure with ships cache and 429 retry

- Cache getShips (paginated, ~9 calls for 847 ships) for 25s so it
  refreshes once per cycle but doesn't re-paginate on back-to-back starts
- Lower RATE_LIMIT_RPS 10 → 7 req/s for more server headroom
- Add 429 retry in requestCore: up to 3 attempts, honouring Retry-After
  header (defaults to 5s/10s/15s backoff if header absent)
- MAX_RETRIES bumped 2 → 3 to match

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix icon circles and layout regressions caused by Tailwind v4 CSS variable opacity bug (#40)

* Initial plan

* fix: fix icon circles, benefit cards, hero buttons, section backgrounds for visual overhaul

Co-authored-by: kaijuthreat <257654705+kaijuthreat@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: kaijuthreat <257654705+kaijuthreat@users.noreply.github.com>

config: increase cycle interval 10s → 20s to reduce rate-limit pressure

Halves the request rate per minute. SELL_IDLE_CYCLES adjusted 18→9
to keep the 3-minute idle-before-sell threshold the same.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

perf: cache static cycle data to reduce rate-limit pressure

Routes, ports, goods, ship types: cached indefinitely (static game data).
Trader positions (NPC prices): cached 5 min / 30 cycles (changes slowly).
Fresh each cycle: ships, company, economy, warehouses, passengers.

Reduces per-cycle API calls from ~10 parallel fetches + 50 inventory
calls to ~5 fresh fetches + 50 inventory calls after first cycle.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: dispatch each ship immediately instead of batching at end

Replace deferred pendingTransits batch flush with per-ship inline
await dispatchTransit() after each routing decision. Ships are
dispatched as soon as their route is determined — no waiting for
all 50 window ships to be processed first.

- Add dispatchTransit(shipId, routeId, onError) helper inside runCycle
- Remove PendingTransit interface, pendingTransits array, TRANSIT_BATCH_SIZE
- Remove batch flush section (loop + console.log for flush)
- All 9 transit call sites now await immediately

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

perf: split ship loop into two passes — traveling tick + window routing

- Pass 1: fast synchronous loop over ALL ships for traveling metric update
- Pass 2: async routing logic over windowShips only (50 ships)
- Removes the 847-iteration outer loop with 790 wasted skip checks
- Loop progress now logs every 10 window ships instead of every 50 total ships
- Removes unused windowShipIds Set

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: rolling ship window — process 50 docked ships per cycle

Instead of processing all docked ships every cycle, iterate a rolling
window of SHIP_WINDOW_SIZE=50 ships (sorted by ID for stability).

- shipWindowOffset stored in AutopilotState, advances each cycle and
  wraps when the full fleet has been covered
- Inventory pre-fetch scoped to window ships only (50 calls vs 490)
- candidatesByPort scoped to window ports only
- Traveling ships still get their cheap metric tick every cycle
- Logs show which window slice is being processed

With 490 docked ships and 10s cycles, full fleet coverage = ~98s (10 cycles).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

debug: add runCycle phase timing logs; raise CYCLE_TIMEOUT_MS to 300s

- [runCycle:fetch] log after data fetch phase
- [runCycle:inv] log after parallel inventory pre-fetch
- [runCycle:cands] log after candidatesByPort pre-compute
- [runCycle:loop] progress every 50 ships + final summary
- [runCycle:flush] transit batch dispatched ok/fail counts + elapsed
- [runCycle:done] total cycle elapsed
- [runCycle:error] log stack trace on catch
- CYCLE_TIMEOUT_MS 60s → 300s (prevents double-cycle with 800 ships)
- Guard fires now logs a console.warn instead of silently resetting

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix visual styling: benefit card icons, hero buttons, service card icon circles (#39)

* Initial plan

* Fix visual styling: benefit card icons, hero buttons, service card icons, dark mode

Co-authored-by: kaijuthreat <257654705+kaijuthreat@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: kaijuthreat <257654705+kaijuthreat@users.noreply.github.com>

perf: memoize pathfinding, batch inventory fetch, pre-compute candidates

- Replace findPaths(x, allRoutes, n) call sites with getPathsFrom(x, n) memoised wrapper
- Pre-compute candidatesByPort once per unique docked port; use in ship loop
- Remove O(n²) coveredPorts rebuild inside ship loop; use pre-computed Set
- Update coveredPorts incrementally after each pax-chase dispatch push
- Remove inline new Date() per passenger; share cycleNow per ship iteration
- Remove stale await fetchShipInv (fetchShipInv is sync from pre-fetched cache)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: resolve init/checkCommands race condition; add targeted debug logging

Race condition: setInterval for checkCommands was at module level (fired
immediately), while void init() ran async. If MongoDB+token fetch took >2s,
checkCommands could enable the worker and call tick() before setWorkerContext
had been called — causing _workerToken/_workerCompanyId to be empty and every
API call to fail with missing header errors.

Fix: wrap init() + setInterval in an async IIFE so polling only starts after
init() fully completes (setWorkerContext already called).

Also:
- Remove duplicate void saveAutopilotState() in tick() (sendState() covers it)
- Add [tick] log: enabled/cycleRunning/token/companyId on every tick call
- Add [setWorkerContext] log when credentials are set
- Add [buildHeaders] warning log when IS_SERVER=true but token/companyId empty
- Add [runCycle] ship count log after data fetch

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: batch transit dispatches in autopilot cycle

Replace all 9 sequential fleetApi.transit() awaits with an optimistic-state
+ pendingTransits queue pattern. After the ship loop, flush all pending
transits in Promise.allSettled batches of TRANSIT_BATCH_SIZE (20).

- State mutations are applied optimistically during the loop so subsequent
  ships see correct coveredPorts / phase data
- onError closures restore pre-dispatch ship state on failure
- Batch flush replaces the sequential per-ship API bottleneck
- Logs total dispatches and batch count after flush

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: move useMemo calls before early return (React error #310)

All hooks must be called unconditionally before any early return.
Moved all useMemo calls above the loading spinner return.
Also slows autopilot poll from 3s to CYCLE_MS (10s).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(ci): bump Go version to 1.25.x in workflow (#140)

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: TwiN <15699766+TwiN@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: thread worker token+companyId through setWorkerContext instead of process.env

Added setWorkerContext(token, companyId) to api/client.ts. buildHeaders()
now prefers these runtime values over process.env, so the standalone worker
no longer needs to mutate process.env to get auth headers on API calls.

The missing 'tradewinds-company-id' header was causing every cycle to fail
with 'getCompany: Missing tradewinds-company-id header'.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

debug: add detailed logging to autopilot init and command polling

Logs:
- MONGODB_URI presence and length
- getDb() connection result and database name
- getAutopilotCredentials() result (email + companyId)
- Per-poll: companyId and command doc found/null

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: pin autopilot cwd to project root so --env-file resolves correctly

pm2 doesn't guarantee the child process CWD matches the ecosystem config
location, so --env-file=.env.local was silently not finding the file.
MONGODB_URI was empty, causing getDb() to return null and all credential
lookups to fail.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:

Fix standalone env loading and missing companyId from credentials

- ecosystem.config.cjs: switch standalone to 'node --env-file=.env.local
  --import tsx' so MONGODB_URI is reliably loaded (pm2 env_file is
  unreliable across pm2 versions)

  breaking resolveCompanyId() fallback in the standalone

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix: standalone stuck on 'unknown' company ID after login

If pm2 boots the standalone before login, companyId stays 'unknown'.
checkCommands() was querying autopilot_commands with companyId='unknown'
(never finds anything) and returning early, so the company ID was never
re-resolved even after login.

Now checkCommands() retries resolveCompanyId() on every poll until it
succeeds, then proceeds to check for commands normally.

Also fix setEnabled/setFleetMgmt to use targeted  writes so they
cannot overwrite each other's fields in the command doc.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Retry token refresh on enable when standalone started before login

If the standalone boots before credentials are stored in MongoDB,
the initial token refresh fails. Now checkCommands() re-attempts
the refresh when it receives an enable command and token is empty.

Also re-resolves companyId if it was unknown at startup.

commit.message:
Remove dev fork, fix stale state on enable/disable

No more child_process fork - both processes run via pm2 in all envs.

When dashboard enables/disables autopilot, we now dual-write:
- autopilot_commands (picked up by standalone in <=2s)
- autopilot_states (read by dashboard on next 3s poll)

This prevents the dashboard from flipping back to stale state
between when the command is written and the standalone applies it.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Restore dev-mode worker fork in autopilot manager

In production pm2 manages autopilot-standalone.ts directly.
In dev, the manager forks it as a child process on first request so
pnpm dev keeps working without any extra steps.

State still flows through MongoDB in both cases - no IPC.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(stdlib): add MPSC channel module (#213)

* feat(stdlib): add MPSC channel module

Expose bounded multi-producer single-consumer channels as
std::channel::channel.  The module provides:

- channel.new(capacity) → (Sender, Receiver)
- Sender: send, clone, close
- Receiver: recv, try_recv, close

The runtime implementation (hew-runtime/src/channel.rs) wraps Rust's
std::sync::mpsc::sync_channel with C-ABI FFI exports.  recv() returns
a valid empty string on channel closure so Hew string comparison works
correctly (NULL ≠ "" in hew_string_equals).

Includes Rust unit tests (5 cases) and an E2E test demonstrating
multi-producer communication with sender cloning.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: merge main and apply formatting

Merge main to pick up recent changes and fix cargo fmt ordering
of pub mod channel declaration in hew-runtime/src/lib.rs.

commit.message:
feat(stdlib): add std::deque module (#210)

Expose a double-ended queue (deque) as an opaque handle type in
the stdlib.  The runtime stores i64 values in a VecDeque behind
a heap-allocated pointer.

New files:
- hew-runtime/src/vecdeque.rs  — #[no_mangle] FFI functions
- std/deque.hew                — opaque Deque type with methods
- E2E test exercising push_front, push_back, pop_front, pop_back,
  len, is_empty, and free

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: hew

commit.message:
feat(stdlib): add std/iter with functional-style Vec<int> operations (#206)

Add std/iter module providing map_int, filter_int, fold_int, any, all,
and sum as pure Hew implementations for Vec<int>. These fill the gap
until a full Iterator trait is available.

Includes E2E test covering all functions plus empty-vector edge cases.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
